topStories.description: Bryan Johnson, a US tech entrepreneur, looking to defy death, said performing squats was better than a 30-minute post-meal walk.

topStories.title: US Army Tests AI Chatbot To Guide Soldiers On Battlefield Decisions
topStories.description: The US Army is developing AI models trained on data from real missions, with the goal of deploying a chatbot specifically for soldiers.

topStories.title: Iran limits Strait of Hormuz transit to 15 ships daily under US ceasefire deal — Report
topStories.description: The Strait — a critical maritime corridor between Iran and Oman — handles about 20% of global oil and key commodities, making any restriction a major concern for global markets.

topStories.title: Has Iran’s 10-point plan changed, as JD Vance claims?
topStories.description: The proposal fuels uncertainty as US officials offer conflicting interpretations of the plan to permanently end hostilities.

topStories.title: Ships remain cautious approaching Strait of Hormuz amid fragile ceasefire
topStories.description: Only a few vessels have crossed the strait since the US-Iran ceasefire deal, according to BBC Verify analysis.

topStories.title: Wisconsin town becomes first in the nation to pass referendum restricting AI data center development
topStories.description: Port Washington, Wisconsin became the first U.S. city to pass a referendum restricting AI data center development after a billion-dollar investment last year.

dailyNews.title: Orissa HC quashes summons against Mukesh Ambani
dailyNews.description: The court penalises a lawyer for repeatedly filing criminal complaints during his 23-year legal battle from a lower court to the Supreme Court over a defective mobile phone worth ₹501

dailyNews.title: Does a US-Iran ceasefire mean the end of the war?
dailyNews.description: After a US-Iran ceasefire deal, strikes slow but tensions remain. Will this hold or collapse?

dailyNews.title: Quote of the day by Kahlil Gibran: ‘Trust in dreams, for in them is….’
dailyNews.description: Kahlil Gibran, a prominent Lebanese-American writer, emphasised the importance of dreams in personal growth and spiritual fulfillment. His quote encourages faith in aspirations as gateways to deeper understanding and purpose, reflecting his belief in the transformative power of inner visions.

dailyNews.description: The conflict has driven up prices of oil, natural gas, damaged refineries and other energy infrastructure.

dailyNews.description: House Democrats reignited 25th Amendment discussion as leadership scheduled a briefing amid calls from members to remove President Trump from office.

dailyNews.title: Unity tested: Democrats face off over Israel and AIPAC dark money during DNC meeting
dailyNews.description: Democrats gather in New Orleans for the DNC spring meeting as internal divisions over Israel policy and AIPAC's role in primaries take center stage.

dailyNews.title: Strait of Hormuz toll would set 'dangerous precedent,' UN shipping agency warns
dailyNews.description: The U.N.'s shipping agency warned on Thursday that any toll imposed on ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz would set a "dangerous precedent."

dailyNews.title: Bodycam shows Athena Strand’s killer FedEx driver flip personas, say discarding girl’s clothes was ‘funny’
dailyNews.description: Haunting body camera footage of FedEx driver Tanner Horner discussing alter ego "Zero" played in Texas courtroom during Athena Strand death penalty trial.

dailyNews.title: Olympic gold winner dismisses athletes' negative comments on representing US: 'I think we're the best country'
dailyNews.description: Jordan Stolz, who won two gold medals in Milan, said he paid no mind to athletes with mixed emotions about representing the U.S. in the Olympics.

dailyNews.title: 'Euphoria' star Natasha Lyonne escorted off plane after appearing disoriented following show premiere: report
dailyNews.description: Natasha Lyonne was reportedly removed from a Delta flight in Los Angeles after allegedly failing to follow crew instructions before takeoff.

dailyNews.title: WNBA star Lexie Hull opens up on pre-game beauty routine, including mandatory naps and nail polish
dailyNews.description: Lexie Hull reveals her WNBA pre-game rituals, from a 20-minute nap to skincare and nails, fueling her confidence heading into her fourth Fever season.

dailyNews.title: Humanoid robots hit mass production in China
dailyNews.description: A Chinese factory is producing humanoid robots every 30 minutes, marking a shift toward large-scale manufacturing and broader adoption.

dailyNews.title: $35 million in reparations money remains unused as Baltimore officials battle over who gets control: report
dailyNews.description: Baltimore officials and reparations commissioners are reportedly feuding over control of $35 million in cannabis tax revenue meant to help communities harmed by the war on drugs.

dailyNews.title: US Marine stabbed to death in North Carolina chaotic street brawl, police hunt person of interest
dailyNews.description: Police search for a person of interest after U.S. Marine Lance Cpl. Daniel Montano was fatally stabbed in a downtown Wilmington, North Carolina, brawl Sunday.

dailyNews.title: Judge describes illegal immigrant burglary crew's cross country operation as 'Ocean's Eleven-style conduct'
dailyNews.description: An "Ocean's Eleven-style" burglary ring of illegal immigrants used GPS trackers and surveillance cameras to target homes across the country, feds say.

topStories.description: The Trump administration has made countering antifa and other far-left groups a counterterrorism priority, despite increasing threats from the Middle East.

topStories.title: How Trump Purged Immigration Judges to Speed Up Deportations
topStories.description: Judges are ordering an unprecedented number of people deported after coming under significant pressure from the administration to do so or risk losing their jobs.

topStories.title: In a Deep Red Town In Pennsylvania, Locals Vent Over a Planned ICE Detention Center
topStories.description: The Tremont, Pa., area has roughly 2,000 residents and limited resources. The Trump administration plans to convert a warehouse there to hold nearly four times as many people.

topStories.description: Democratic politicians are swearing with glee. It is usually aimed at President Trump.

topStories.description: A growing chorus of disaffected Trump supporters is sounding off in the replies to his posts on the social media platform he founded.
